content.boundary: Applies to the existing admissions, maintainer, and track structure nationally; excludes any new admissions rule, capacity, or curriculum change.
content.change_lever: No structural lever exercised; reliance on existing assessment infrastructure (National ABC, PISA) for monitoring and voluntary future action.
content.name: Status quo with monitoring (passive counterfactual)
content.system_problem: Hungary shows the EU's highest socioeconomic and Roma school segregation, with 71% of reading variance explained between schools versus 36% OECD average, and no discrete legislative proposal currently targets the tracks' structural existence.
